data.text: having or showing the insight, intelligence, and understanding to make good judgments about practical matters (as in business or finance); also: wily, cunning
examples: a shrewd investor
made some shrewd business moves
a coach with a shrewd eye for talent
… a shrewd operator who transported arms for years and was finally undone by a … crackdown …
… I got some shrewd knocks over it, but I did win this much.
Touch it if you will, it gives out shrewd galvanic shocks …
a shrewd wind
… the first shrewd gust of a storm pressed the craft against the bank.
